issues.title: Loan Simple leads receiving double tasks in post-referral and other workflows
issues.desc: Loan Simple leads were triggering duplicate tasks across multiple workflows including pre-referral and post-referral. The issue was caused by a bugged filter related to Loan Simple lead associations in HubSpot. Madelaine worked with HubSpot support to resolve the filter problem.
issues.workaround: Review any LS leads that received duplicate tasks during the impact window and manually delete the duplicate tasks from their records.

issues.title: Emails showing previous loan officer after LO change on deal
issues.desc: After a loan officer was changed on a deal, automated emails continued to reference the previous LO rather than the newly assigned one. The affected deal was showing Paul instead of Julie Yocum in outbound agent emails. Luis investigated and resolved the issue.
issues.workaround: For deals where the LO was changed during the impact window, manually resend any automated agent-facing emails that referenced the incorrect loan officer.

issues.title: Post-referral workflow tasks not stopping when deal marked Closed Lost or Transferred
issues.desc: The post-referral workflow continued generating check-in tasks for deals that had been marked as Closed Lost or Transferred, rather than stopping enrollment. Home advisors reported receiving weekly check-in tasks on lost deals. Madelaine made a tweak to the workflow filters to prevent tasks from firing on these deal stages.
issues.workaround: Manually dismiss any check-in tasks that were incorrectly generated for deals marked Closed Lost or Transferred during the impact window.

issues.title: Offer Accepted workflow not re-triggering after deal stage flipped back and forth
issues.desc: When a deal was moved to Offer Accepted, then back to Referred Active or Committed after a contract termination, and then back to Offer Accepted again, the TC workflow tasks did not re-trigger on the second Offer Accepted update. The root cause was that deal stage Offer Accepted was not listed as a re-enrollment criterion in the workflow. Madelaine added it as a re-enrollment trigger.
issues.workaround: For any deals that went back to Offer Accepted during the impact window and did not receive TC tasks, manually enroll them in the Offer Accepted workflow to generate the closing info collection tasks.

issues.title: MRC deals updated to Offer Accepted not enrolling TCs into workflow tasks
issues.desc: MRC deals being updated to Offer Accepted were not triggering the TC workflow, meaning TCs were not receiving tasks to collect closing information. The issue was investigated with HubSpot support and resolved; MRC deals typically have no loan officer assigned, which was initially suspected but confirmed not to be the root cause. HubSpot support helped identify and fix the underlying trigger issue.
issues.workaround: Manually enroll any MRC deals updated to Offer Accepted between approximately March 14 and April 1 into the TC workflow to generate closing info collection tasks.
